name: control_phone
description: Make the user's OWN phone perform a task by delegating it to the Gemma 4 AI model running ON that phone — the BlackBox wakes that on-device model over Tailscale and it actuates the device. THIS is the tool for requests like 'do X on my phone', 'use my phone to ...', 'have my phone open/show/check ...': it drives the phone's own on-device Gemma 4, which is DIFFERENT from server-side or remote-browser device-control tools. Capabilities: open apps, navigate the UI, read the screen, set a timer, show a map, run a web search, toggle the flashlight, take a photo. YOU MUST ACTUALLY CALL THIS TOOL (do not merely say that you will) AND WAIT FOR ITS RESULT before you reply — it BLOCKS while the phone WAKES Gemma (a cold model load, up to ~75s) and runs the task, then returns what Gemma actually did. Recommended: tell the user 'Waking Gemma on your phone — I'll report back', then wait for the tool result. Only SAFE actions run remotely; high-consequence actions (send SMS/email, place calls, payments) are refused and must be done by the user directly. Returns Gemma's result text, or a structured error (phone offline / wake failed / timed out / refused) so you can retry or stop.

task.description: The natural-language task to run on the phone, e.g. 'open Google Maps and search for coffee near me' or 'what's on my screen right now?'.

returns: The result text reported by the on-device model, or a structured error (error_kind: no_device / wake_failed / refused / bad_response / lost_contact / remote_error / timeout).
notes: Frontier->phone inversion: the BlackBox reaches out to the originating operator's phone (resolved via the tailnet mesh) and drives its on-device Gemma. Blocking with wake-tracking; the phone enforces a remote allowlist. v1 targets the originating operator's device; Phase 2 adds an optional device param. See docs/plans/2026-06-18-frontier-to-phone-control.md.
